slc_intro: First measure whether the lag is on the server (A) or your phone's screen (B), then honestly handle the client half. It does not intercept fetch, touch chat rendering, or fix the server — the frontend physically can't.
slc_gauge_note: Off by default; samples for 10 seconds when pressed — scroll the chat normally. Measures only this phone (B), not the server (A).
slc_nudge_enabled: Suggest starting a new chat when it gets long
slc_ops_lead: Most real fixes for cloud lag live on the server/ops side; a frontend extension can only assist and diagnose. Ordered by the plan's ROI:
slc_ops_1: Install cocktail-plus (frontend + backend) — the plan's core recommendation: backend cache, early-bridge startup speedup, save patching, parallel extension activation. Requires enableServerPlugins: true in config.yaml plus copying the backend component (plan section 4). Pair with BaiBai-Tools for phone-side fetch/DOM optimization.
slc_ops_2: Set a memory limit on the Pod so the platform's OOMKill auto-restarts it, replacing the fragile pre-dawn cron; or schedule staggered pre-dawn restarts to zero out memory.
slc_ops_3: config.yaml tuning: enable lazyLoadCharacters, enable requestCompression, memoryCacheCapacity: 50mb, disable enableServerPluginsAutoUpdate.
slc_ops_4: Disk cleanup: periodically clear character cache _cache/characters/, prune overflow backups/, shallow-clone extension .git histories, delete unused vector indexes.
slc_ops_5: Frontend: turn off "auto-load last chat", start a new chat when it gets long, remove cloud-useless extensions (TauriTavern etc.). The health check above can do these in one click.
slc_ops_foot: See the Cloud Tavern Optimization Plan for details. This extension tells you where the problem is and one-click-fixes what it safely can — but it will not, and cannot, touch the server (reading /proc, clearing disk, restarting all need server privileges).
